input_dict.question: Ronnie Dunn and Jenna Sanz-Agero were both formerly part of bands in which they each acted in what role?
input_dict.source_summary: Ronnie Dunn and Jenna Sanz-Agero both served as vocalists (lead singers) in their respective bands. Jenna Sanz-Agero was the former lead singer of the female rock group Vixen. Ronnie Dunn is an American country music singer who sang lead vocals for the duo Brooks & Dunn on songs such as "Only in America".
input_dict.target_summary: From the first-hop context: Jenna Sanz-Agero is the former lead singer of the female rock group Vixen. Brooks & Dunn is a duo consisting of Kix Brooks and Ronnie Dunn, with both members described as vocalists and songwriters. Visible second-hop evidence: Ronnie Gene Dunn is an American country music singer-songwriter and record executive. Integration/bridge note: Jenna's role as Vixen's lead singer is confirmed; Ronnie Dunn is confirmed as a singer-songwriter and as one half of a vocal duo, but the materials available at this boundary do not establish whether he held the specific title "lead singer" for Brooks & Dunn.
input_dict.candidate_summary: From the first-hop context: Jenna Sanz-Agero is the former lead singer of the female rock group Vixen. Brooks & Dunn is a duo consisting of Kix Brooks and Ronnie Dunn, with both members described as vocalists and songwriters. Visible second-hop evidence: Ronnie Gene Dunn is an American country music singer-songwriter and record executive. Integration/bridge note: Jenna's role as Vixen's lead singer is confirmed; Ronnie Dunn is confirmed as a singer-songwriter and as one half of a vocal duo, but the materials available at this boundary do not establish whether he held the specific title "lead singer" for Brooks & Dunn.
